text.heading: Curb area boys manace! Mainland residents demand
text.p: The menace that area boys and government touts constitute to the society was in sharp focus at the town hall meeting by the Lagos State House of Assembly held for residents of Mainland constituencies 1 and II at Hoarse Methodist Memorial Cathedral Hall, Yaba.

A resident Prince Soji Kabiawo said that the menace of social miscreants popularly referred to as area boys was too much in the area and called for urgent intervention of the Assembly. Contributing, Mrs Ajoke Olawuye also lamented that it was difficult to do things in the area without one form of harassment or the other.

While Mr Taiwo Obashola representative of the Rector, Mr Olubunmi Owoso, Yaba College of Technology called on the Assembly to enact a law to stem the menace of local government touts who posed as traffic control officials in the area. He continued that the touts posed as traffic experts as they stayed on the road in the guise of directing traffic. Adding that, they lacked training in traffic laws.

According to him "If you slow down they ask question. If you are fast they ask questions. "The next thing they do is to enter your vehicle and ask you to drive down to the council over unspecified traffic offences he said. He called on the Assembly to enact a law to take care of their menace as they were "causing a lot of embarrassment" in the area.
The residents also asked the government to improve social amenities and create employment opportunities for the youth in the area.

Mr Tom Ayakri, representative of Queens College said that the school was cut off from water supply and pleaded that they be linked up to the state water corporation. He also pleaded that refuse disposal services be extended to the school.

Residents of Ebute whose shanties were spreading towards the third mainland bridge also appealed to the state government to have to mercy and not carry out the proposed demolition of the shanties. Other demands include road rehabilitation at Abule Ijesha, Rice Road and other roads in the areas as well as construction of drainages to stop flooding during the rainy season.

The residents also demanded that the menace of estate agents who defrauded unsuspecting accommodation seekers be checked. They also demanded for construction of pedestrian bridge, stoppage of street trading, and urgent relocation of saw millers to permanent site, among others.
Earlier in his address, Speaker of the Lagos State House of Assembly, Mr Adeyemi Ikuforji explained to the constituents the role of the legislature in the a democratic dispensation.

Ikuforiji said that the town hall meeting was organized to provide an avenue for constituents to channel their actual need so that it can be communicated to the executive arm to ensure projects executed were in line with the people’s needs.

"The legislature is not the arm of government that implements government policies and programmes but it is in position to offer the executive arm of government the information that could lead to a considerable reduction in poverty level among our constituents," he said.
Giving the welcome address, Mr Jide Jimoh, chairman of Yaba Local Council Development Authority, commended the Assembly for taking the initiative which was the first in the nation.

Jimoh said that governance had gone beyond a level where politicians "sat in their offices" to form policies for the people they represent. He said that laws were not made in a "vacuum" and that the trend has changed with the people now wanting to know how they were governed.
